title: Parameterize a Build With ARG
description:
## Pass a Value at Build Time

`ARG` declares a variable available **only during the build**, settable with `--build-arg`. A build context is prepared at **`/root/arglab`** with this `Dockerfile`:

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest
ARG GREETING=default
RUN echo "$GREETING" > /greeting.txt
CMD ["cat", "/greeting.txt"]
```

**Requirements:**
1. Build the image tagged **`greeter:1.0`**
2. Override the `GREETING` build arg so the baked-in file contains **`kubekosh`**

> 💡 `cd /root/arglab && docker build --build-arg GREETING=kubekosh -t greeter:1.0 .`

hints.title: Pass a build arg
hints.body: --build-arg NAME=value sets an ARG declared in the Dockerfile for that build.

hints.title: Verify the baked-in value
hints.body: Running the image prints the greeting that was written at build time.

validation.description: Checks that greeter:1.0 was built with GREETING=kubekosh baked in.
